title: MD
biography: Charles Best was a Canadian medical scientist who co-discovered insulin with Frederick Banting in 1921. As a 22-year-old medical student, he assisted Banting in the experiments that led to the isolation and purification of insulin, saving millions of lives from diabetes.
aiSummary: Charles Best was a physiology specialist at the University of Toronto who co-discovered insulin with Frederick Banting in 1921. His work saved millions of diabetics. Based in Toronto, Canada.

affiliations.role: Professor of Physiology and Director of Banting-Best Department

citations.title: The internal secretion of the pancreas

awards: Order of the Companions of Honour

timeline.title: Co-discovered insulin
timeline.description: With Frederick Banting, isolated insulin from dog pancreas and demonstrated its ability to lower blood sugar

timeline.title: First human insulin treatment
timeline.description: Insulin was successfully administered to Leonard Thompson, the first diabetic patient treated

timeline.title: Director of Banting-Best Department of Medical Research
timeline.description: Led the research department at the University of Toronto
